implementationArtifact.specBody:
---
complexity: low
---

# Feature: Add Footer

## Summary

Add a simple footer to the bottom of every page with copyright text.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Footer appears at the bottom of the page
- [ ] Footer contains copyright text with the current year and "BrewBoard"

## Technical Approach

Add a footer element to `src/app/page.tsx`. One line, no complex styling needed.

## Out of Scope

- Do NOT write tests
- Do NOT add documentation
- Do NOT refactor existing code

## Validation

```bash
echo "Feature Add Footer validated"
```

quality.summary: Near-perfect implementation of a trivial spec. Three lines added to page.tsx satisfy both acceptance criteria exactly: semantic <footer> element, dynamic copyright year, and 'BrewBoard' text. Tailwind classes are minimal and appropriate. The only non-perfect mark is the empty log file (boilerplate Aigon workflow artifact), which is a workflow system expectation rather than product churn.

requirements.notes: Both acceptance criteria met: footer appears at bottom of page (placed after beer cards, before </main>) and contains copyright text with current year and 'BrewBoard'. Added to the correct file (src/app/page.tsx). No tests, docs, or refactors added.

correctness.notes: JSX is valid. © renders © correctly. new Date().getFullYear() correctly produces the current year at render time. Footer placement inside <main> after the grid div is semantically and structurally correct. Tailwind classes (mt-8, text-center, text-sm, text-stone-500) are all standard and appropriate.

minimality.notes: 3-line change to page.tsx is exactly right. Log file is expected Aigon workflow scaffolding, not product churn. Minor knock: log sections are all empty, which is technically noise, but this is a system-level artifact rather than a code quality issue.

code_quality.notes: Semantic <footer> element used rather than a bare <div>. Tailwind classes are consistent with the apparent project style. Dynamic year generation follows best practice. Code is immediately readable with no explanation needed.

risk.notes: Minimal additive change with zero refactoring. Semantic HTML improves accessibility. Visually unobtrusive styling (small, muted text) poses no regression risk to existing UI. No new dependencies.
